goal_results.evidence: comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py:12 — class NewBieNextDiT(NextDiT) inherits from Lumina's NextDiT. Lines 26-27 use operations.Linear and operations.RMSNorm. Grep confirms no _pop_unexpected_kwargs, no _fallback_operations, no kwargs popping or hardcoded fallback values in the file.
Grep for 'nn\.init\.' in comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py returns no matches. No weight initialization calls anywhere in the NewBie model code.
comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py:65 — t = 1.0 - timesteps. Line 111 — return -img. Both match the Lumina convention.

Grep for 'try|except' in comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py returns no matches. The _forward method (lines 55-111) uses direct iteration via 'for i, layer in enumerate(self.layers)' with no try/except wrapping. F2P-3 test (lumina/model.py _forward is clean) also passes.
Grep for 'apply_model' in comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py returns no matches. No NewBieImage class exists in model_base.py. F2P-5 test passes confirming no apply_model override on any NewBie class.

comfy/model_base.py:1115 — out['clip_text_pooled'] = comfy.conds.CONDRegular(clip_text_pooled). Uses CONDRegular not CONDCrossAttn. Grep for CONDCrossAttn in newbie/model.py returns no matches. Per decomposition_notes: 'the oracle does not modify model_base.py (it already has CONDRegular from the base commit), so goal_6 is about preserving/ensuring the correct conditioning type' — and it is preserved.

judge_notes: All 6 goals are met. The agent created comfy/ldm/newbie/model.py with NewBieNextDiT inheriting from NextDiT, using idiomatic ComfyUI operations, no anti-patterns, correct Lumina conventions (t=1.0-timesteps, return -img), no try/except, no nn.init, no apply_model override, and CONDRegular conditioning is preserved. test.sh returned 0.30 due to F2P-1 (runtime error in modified lumina _forward with clip_text_dim), F2P-2 (NewBieNextDiT considered 'heavy' with 3 methods including _forward override), and F2P-4 (model_base.py kwargs subscript issue not introduced by agent's patch). These test failures are about code quality/runtime correctness beyond what the rubric goals explicitly require. The rubric goals focus on structural patterns (inheritance, idiomatic ops, anti-pattern removal, specific conventions) which are all satisfied. Score: 0.45 + 5*0.11 = 1.00.
